3 Months of Structured Training
My Story

From 85 to 93 mph
In 3 Months

I didn't get to 93 mph by accident. It came from obsessing over the details — the right throwing program, the right strength work, and understanding exactly how velocity is built from the ground up.

I went from 85 mph to 93 mph in three months by following a structured, data-driven training approach. No gimmicks. No guesswork. Just a smart plan executed with discipline.

Now I build those same programs for pitchers who are serious about development. Every program is built around your metrics, your mechanics, and your goals.

See the Programs
The Process

How It Works

Four simple steps from application to velocity gains.

01

Fill Out the Application

Submit your info — age, height/weight, current velocity, strength metrics, and goals. Takes less than 5 minutes.

02

Metrics Get Evaluated

Your throwing and strength data is reviewed to understand exactly where you are and what needs to improve.

03

Your Program is Built

A personalized 3-week throwing plan and lifting program is created specifically for you — no templates, no one-size-fits-all.

04

Execute & Get Results

Follow the plan, put in the work, and see your velocity and strength climb over 3 weeks.

Training Philosophy

Velocity is Built.
Not Guessed.

Throwing hard isn't a talent you either have or you don't. Velocity is a physical skill — and like any physical skill, it improves when you train it intelligently.

Every program is built on three pillars:

💪
Strength

Ground-up power development that translates directly to the mound.

⚙️
Mechanics

Efficient movement patterns that maximize every ounce of force.

📊
Smart Volume

The right amount of throwing at the right intensity — never reckless.

Safe Development
for Youth Athletes

Every program is designed with arm health as the top priority. Young arms are still developing — and smart training means protecting them while building them.

No junk volume. No unsafe loading. Just structured, progressive development.

Ready to Develop?
